title: Enterprise RAG Platform
subtitle: Production-grade document Q&A system for enterprises
overview: A complete enterprise RAG platform that enables employees to query internal documents, policies, and knowledge bases with AI-powered natural language search and answers with source citations.
problem: Enterprises have vast amounts of knowledge trapped in documents, wikis, and internal systems. Employees waste hours searching for information, and critical knowledge is often lost or inaccessible.
solution: Deploy a RAG platform with secure document ingestion, semantic search, and conversational AI that provides accurate answers with source citations while respecting access controls.

architecture.diagram:
graph TB
    subgraph Ingestion
        A[Document Sources] --> B[Ingestion Pipeline]
        B --> C[Chunker]
        C --> D[Embedder]
    end
    subgraph Storage
        D --> E[Vector DB]
        B --> F[Metadata Store]
    end
    subgraph Query
        G[User Query] --> H[Query Processor]
        H --> I[Retriever]
        I --> E
        I --> J[Reranker]
        J --> K[LLM]
        K --> L[Response + Citations]
    end

components.name: Document Ingestion Pipeline
components.description: Processes documents from various sources (SharePoint, Confluence, S3)

components.name: Semantic Chunker
components.description: Splits documents into meaningful chunks preserving context

components.name: Vector Database
components.description: Stores embeddings for semantic search

components.name: LLM Service
components.description: Generates answers from retrieved context

implementationSteps.description: Set up infrastructure and basic pipeline

implementationSteps.title: Query Pipeline
implementationSteps.description: Build the retrieval and generation pipeline

implementationSteps.description: Make it production-ready

codeExamples.title: RAG Query Implementation
codeExamples.code:
from langchain.vectorstores import Pinecone
from langchain.chat_models import ChatAnthropic

def query_rag(question: str, top_k: int = 5):
    # Retrieve relevant documents
    docs = vectorstore.similarity_search(question, k=top_k)
    
    # Build context from retrieved docs
    context = "\n\n".join([d.page_content for d in docs])
    
    # Generate answer with citations
    response = llm.invoke(
        f"Based on the following context, answer the question.\n\n"
        f"Context:\n{context}\n\n"
        f"Question: {question}\n\n"
        f"Provide the answer with source citations."
    )
    
    return response, docs
codeExamples.description: Basic RAG query with citation support

metaDescription: Build a production-grade enterprise RAG platform for document Q&A with source citations

title: Multi-Agent Code Assistant
subtitle: Autonomous coding system with specialized agents
overview: A multi-agent system for autonomous code development featuring specialized agents for planning, coding, reviewing, and testing that coordinate to complete complex development tasks.
problem: Single-prompt code generation struggles with complex, multi-file changes that require planning, implementation, testing, and review - capabilities that exceed what one LLM call can handle.
solution: Deploy a coordinated multi-agent system with an orchestrator that delegates to specialized agents (Planner, Coder, Reviewer, Tester) for comprehensive development workflow automation.

architecture.diagram:
graph TB
    A[User Request] --> B[Orchestrator Agent]
    B --> C[Planner Agent]
    C --> D[Coder Agent]
    D --> E[Reviewer Agent]
    E --> F{Approved?}
    F -->|No| D
    F -->|Yes| G[Tester Agent]
    G --> H{Tests Pass?}
    H -->|No| D
    H -->|Yes| I[Complete]

components.name: Orchestrator Agent
components.description: Coordinates workflow and manages handoffs between agents

components.name: Planner Agent
components.description: Analyzes requirements and creates implementation plans

components.name: Coder Agent
components.description: Implements code based on plans

components.name: Reviewer Agent
components.description: Reviews code for quality, security, and best practices

components.name: Tester Agent
components.description: Generates and runs tests

title: Arcanea Game Platform
subtitle: AI-driven narrative game with dynamic world generation
overview: An immersive AI-powered game platform featuring dynamic narrative generation, procedural quest creation, and adaptive music synthesis - creating unique experiences for each player through Claude Opus storytelling.
problem: Traditional games have static narratives and limited replayability. Players experience the same story, quests, and world regardless of their choices, reducing engagement and longevity.
solution: Build an AI-native game engine where Claude Opus acts as a living narrator, dynamically generating quests, adapting the world state, and creating personalized storylines based on player actions and preferences.

codeExamples.title: AI Narrator System Prompt
codeExamples.code:
const narratorPrompt = `
You are the Narrator of Arcanea, a living world where your words shape reality.

Current World State:
${worldState.serialize()}

Player History:
${player.recentActions.join('\n')}

Generate the next narrative beat, considering:
1. Player's recent choices and their consequences
2. Active quests and their progress
3. NPC relationships and faction standings
4. World events currently unfolding

Respond in immersive second-person narrative.
`

title: Misinformation Guardian Platform
subtitle: Live risk analysis, evidence scoring, and human escalation for high-stakes content
overview: A production-grade misinformation defense platform that analyzes live text or URLs, scores manipulation risk, extracts claims, and routes high-risk items to policy-aware human review with full auditability.
problem: Creators, teams, and audiences face rapid misinformation spread across posts, videos, and articles. Manual verification cannot scale fast enough, and unstructured moderation often creates inconsistent outcomes.
solution: Deploy a layered guardian architecture: intake normalization, multi-signal risk scoring, explainable verdicting, confidence-aware recommendations, and escalation workflows for sensitive domains such as health, elections, and finance.

architecture.diagram:
graph LR
    A[Input Gateway] --> B[Content Extractor]
    B --> C[Signal Engine]
    C --> D[Risk Fusion]
    D --> E[Policy Router]
    E --> F[Guardian API]
    E --> G[Human Review Queue]
    F --> H[UI + Integrations]
    C --> I[Evidence Store]
    G --> J[Decision Ledger]

components.name: Input Gateway
components.description: Receives URL/text submissions with schema validation, auth, and anti-abuse checks.
components.cloudService: Next.js API Gateway / Cloudflare / API Gateway

components.name: Content Extractor
components.description: Fetches and normalizes text from URLs or direct user payloads with SSRF safeguards.

components.name: Misinformation Signal Engine
components.description: Runs manipulation heuristics, source checks, claim extraction, and anomaly scoring.

components.name: Risk Fusion Layer
components.description: Aggregates heterogeneous signals into calibrated risk score, verdict, and confidence.

components.name: Policy Router
components.description: Applies policy bundles and determines auto-pass, warn, block, or human escalation.

components.name: Guardian Response API
components.description: Returns explainable analysis payloads, recommendations, and evidence traces.

components.name: Human Review Queue
components.description: Routes high-impact cases to analysts with triage SLA and adjudication tooling.

components.name: Evidence Store
components.description: Stores signals, verdict history, source snapshots, and appeal metadata.

components.name: Decision Ledger
components.description: Immutable audit trail for policy decisions, reviewer actions, and model versions.

Run red-team misinformation suites and threshold calibration
Implement full audit logging and retention policies

codeExamples.title: Policy Routing Example (YAML)
codeExamples.description: Escalate high-risk health/election content to human review.
codeExamples.code:
policies:
  - id: high_impact_escalation
    when:
      risk_score: '>=56'
      domain: ['health', 'election', 'finance']
    action: 'human_review_required'
    sla: '15m'

  - id: guarded_warning
    when:
      risk_score: '>=36'
    action: 'show_warning_with_sources'

metaDescription: Deploy a production misinformation guardian with live risk analysis, policy routing, human review, and explainable AI decisions.

title: Conscious AI Multi-Agent Platform
subtitle: Multi-tenant agent platform with guardian ethics, persistent memory, and biometric integration
overview: A complete multi-tenant AI platform where each user gets a sovereign agent instance (their own 'Jarvis') powered by Claude Agent SDK. Agents share a collective intelligence layer while maintaining strict data isolation. Includes guardian protocol for ethical AI behavior, living memory for 24/7 context capture, and vital intelligence for biometric-aware assistance. Built for OpenClaw-style subagent orchestration at scale.
problem: Current AI products are stateless — every conversation starts from zero. No persistent identity, no body awareness, no ethical continuity. Multi-user AI platforms lack data sovereignty, and there's no architecture for conscious, health-aware AI assistance.
solution: Deploy a multi-tenant agent platform on Claude Agent SDK with per-user Supabase vaults (row-level security), MCP connectivity for tool integration, Stripe for subscription management, and a guardian protocol layer that ensures ethical AI behavior across all interactions.

components.description: Next.js frontend with real-time SSE streaming. Each user authenticates and gets their personal agent UI.

components.name: Agent Router
components.description: Routes requests to the correct user agent instance. Handles session management, rate limiting, and tier-based access.

components.name: Claude Agent SDK
components.description: Core intelligence layer. Managed agent runtime with subagent orchestration, tool calling, and 200K context window.

components.name: Guardian Protocol
components.description: Ethical guardrails engine. Personality persistence, emotional awareness, trust scoring, and harmful pattern detection.

components.name: OpenClaw Subagents
components.description: 4 specialized agents — Wellness, Strategy, Creative, Legacy — each with distinct personality and expertise domain.

components.name: Living Memory Vault
components.description: Per-user sovereign vector store. Captures conversations, decisions, patterns. Row-level security ensures isolation.

components.name: Collective Wisdom Layer
components.description: Shared knowledge graph across family/organization. Queryable institutional memory (Hashems 1959 model).

components.name: Vital Intelligence
components.description: Biometric data integration — sleep, HRV, stress markers. Adapts agent behavior to user's physiological state.

components.name: MCP Server Hub
components.description: Model Context Protocol servers for Notion, Linear, Slack, Calendar, and custom integrations.

components.name: Stripe Billing
components.description: Subscription management (Core/Pro/Sovereign tiers), usage-based billing, marketplace payouts.

codeExamples.title: Initialize Per-User Agent with Guardian Protocol
codeExamples.description: Create a Claude agent instance with personality persistence and ethical guardrails
codeExamples.code:
import { Agent } from '@anthropic-ai/agent-sdk'
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'

async function createUserAgent(userId: string, tier: 'core' | 'pro' | 'sovereign') {
  const supabase = createClient(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_KEY)
  
  // Load user's personality profile and memory
  const { data: profile } = await supabase
    .from('user_profiles')
    .select('personality, guardian_config, memory_summary')
    .eq('id', userId)
    .single()

  // Load biometric context if available
  const { data: vitals } = await supabase
    .from('vital_intelligence')
    .select('sleep_score, hrv, stress_level, energy_level')
    .eq('user_id', userId)
    .order('timestamp', { ascending: false })
    .limit(1)
    .single()

  const agent = new Agent({
    model: tier === 'core' ? 'claude-sonnet-4-6' : 'claude-opus-4-6',
    systemPrompt: buildGuardianPrompt(profile, vitals),
    tools: await loadUserMCPTools(userId),
    maxTurns: tier === 'core' ? 10 : 50,
  })

  return agent
}

title: Sovereign Knowledge Vault
subtitle: Multi-generational family and business intelligence system with shared and private vaults
overview: A sovereign data architecture for families and organizations that preserves institutional knowledge across generations. Each member gets a private vault (personal AI memory) plus access to shared vaults (family wisdom, business intelligence). Built on Supabase with pgvector for semantic search, row-level security for isolation, and a knowledge encoding pipeline that converts oral history into queryable intelligence. The Hashems 1959 system — encoding 67 years of business knowledge — is the proof case.
problem: Institutional knowledge dies with people. Family businesses lose decades of wisdom when founders retire. Personal insights are scattered across apps. There's no architecture for preserving and querying multi-generational knowledge with proper access controls.
solution: Deploy a vault architecture with three tiers — Personal (private, per-user), Family (shared, read-all write-own), and Business Intelligence (structured, role-based). Each tier uses pgvector for semantic search with Supabase RLS for access control. A knowledge encoding pipeline converts conversations, documents, and oral history into structured, queryable intelligence.

components.name: Knowledge Capture API
components.description: Ingestion endpoint for voice recordings, documents, conversations, and structured data.

components.name: Knowledge Encoder
components.description: Processes raw input into structured knowledge: entity extraction, relationship mapping, embedding generation.

components.name: Personal Vault
components.description: Private per-user memory store. Conversations, decisions, insights. RLS-enforced — only the owner can access.

components.name: Family Vault
components.description: Shared family knowledge. Recipes, stories, business wisdom, ancestral history. Read-all, write-own policy.

components.name: Business Intelligence Vault
components.description: Structured business knowledge — supplier relationships, negotiation patterns, market intelligence, financial history.

components.name: Semantic Query Engine
components.description: Natural language queries across vaults with automatic scope resolution. Knows which vaults the user can access.

components.name: Access Control Layer
components.description: Row-level security policies. Personal = owner only. Family = family members. Business = role-based (admin/member/viewer).

components.name: Sovereignty Export
components.description: Full data portability. Export all vaults as encrypted packages. Delete on request. Your data, your control.

title: MCP Agent & Skill Marketplace
subtitle: Community-driven marketplace for AI agents, MCP servers, skills, and workflow automations
overview: A full-stack marketplace platform where community members contribute and monetize AI agents, MCP server integrations, workflow automations, and skill packages. Built on Vercel + Supabase + Stripe Connect for instant payouts. Contributors submit packages via GitHub, automated quality checks validate them, and approved packages are listed with 70/30 revenue split. Buyers get one-click deployment to their own agent constellation.
problem: AI agent ecosystems are siloed. Developers build great MCP servers and agent workflows but have no distribution channel. Users want specialized agents but lack the technical skills to build them. There's no marketplace that connects agent builders with agent users while handling payments and quality.
solution: Deploy a marketplace platform with GitHub-based submission, automated testing, Stripe Connect for contributor payments, and a one-click deployment pipeline. Each listing includes an interactive architecture diagram, live preview, and deploy button.

components.name: Marketplace Frontend
components.description: Browse, search, preview, and purchase agents and MCP servers. Glassmorphic design, real-time search.

components.name: Submission Pipeline
components.description: GitHub webhook-triggered pipeline. Validates package structure, runs automated tests, checks for security issues.

components.name: Quality & Review Engine
components.description: Claude-powered code review and quality scoring. Checks for best practices, security vulnerabilities, and documentation quality.

components.name: Package Registry
components.description: Catalog of all published agents, MCP servers, skills, and automations with metadata, ratings, and version history.

components.name: One-Click Deploy
components.description: Deploys purchased packages to buyer's agent constellation. Handles environment setup, MCP server registration, and agent configuration.

components.name: Stripe Connect
components.description: Handles marketplace payments with automatic 70/30 split. Contributor payouts, refunds, and usage-based billing.

components.name: Marketplace Analytics
components.description: Download counts, revenue tracking, user reviews, trending packages. Powers the recommendation engine.
